title: Hungarian Wine Climate Atlas — district-level climate susceptibility, variety suitability and threats for the 22 Hungarian wine districts (1971–2100)
description:
An open-source bilingual (Hungarian + English) information site that visualises climate-change susceptibility for the 22 Hungarian wine districts (borvidék). It joins three peer-reviewed climate datasets to a curated knowledge base of 58 grape varieties and surfaces the result as a publishable, citation-ready website.

What is in here:

  • A Python pipeline (analysis/src/) that extracts daily climate fields from the FORESEE v1.1 regional dataset (Dobor et al. 2015) at district level via area-weighted polygon means, computes nine viticulture indices (Winkler GDD, Huglin Index, frost days, heat-stress days, Hargreaves PET drought balance, Cool Night Index, etc.), and validates against the World Bank CCKP CMIP6 0.25° ensemble.
  • A variety-suitability matcher with trapezoidal Huglin scoring, soft Winkler-class penalty, and frost/heat tolerance penalties, evaluated across four future bins (2021-2040, 2041-2060, 2061-2080, 2081-2100) and two scenarios (RCP4.5, RCP8.5).
  • A curated threats dataset covering the active 2025 Flavescence dorée outbreak (NÉBIH detected in 21 of 22 wine districts), grapevine trunk diseases, late spring frost events, and EU pesticide regulation status.
  • 22 long-form research dossiers (~123,000 words) and two synthesis briefs, fully translated to Hungarian.
  • A bilingual Next.js + MapLibre + deck.gl static site (HU + EN) with 1,091 prerendered pages, including per-district detail pages, side-by-side district comparisons, threat-ranking views, methods explainers, downloadable per-district PDFs, and a sortable comparison table.
  • SHA-256 checksums and a versioned manifest.json for the curated data bundle.

Who is it for:

  • Hungarian and international wine investors evaluating district-level climate risk
  • Wine growers and hegyközségi tanácsadók considering variety adaptation
  • Climate impact researchers needing a reproducible Hungarian baseline
  • Policy makers and the wine-curious public

Reproducibility: Every site chart can be re-derived from the curated parquet/JSON/GeoJSON in analysis/curated/. The Python pipeline is idempotent and re-runnable; the Next.js site builds to a static export deployable to any host.
